methodology: Patterns identified from sample-scrape.json (32 pages across 27 archetypes), mapped to EDS Block Collection (https://www.aem.live/developer/block-collection) and Block Party. Block Collection verified via live nav-page search; Block Party searched for specialized patterns (breadcrumb, filter, stats, sidebar, profile, testimonial, form embed).

breadcrumb.title: Breadcrumb
breadcrumb.description: Creates a breadcrumb nav based on page titles of parent pages
breadcrumb.author: shsteimer

content-filter-hub.title: Content Filter Hub
content-filter-hub.description: Search, filter, sort and explore - find exactly what you're looking for with powerful real-time content filtering.
content-filter-hub.author: rusmeenkhan1

blocks.name: hero
blocks.description: Full-width banner with background image, gradient overlay, heading (h1), subtitle paragraph, and optional CTA button. Used as the primary page introduction on nearly every page. The homepage version features a multi-slide carousel (Slick.js on source site), but most inner pages use a single static hero.

variants.name: hero (default)
variants.description: Static single-image hero with heading, subtitle, and CTA button

variants.name: hero (carousel)
variants.description: Multi-slide hero using Block Collection Carousel wrapper or nested carousel. Homepage has 3-5 rotating slides, each with image + text + CTA
variants.notes: Consider implementing as Carousel block with hero-styled slides rather than modifying the hero block itself

variants.name: hero (profile)
variants.description: Hero with person's name, title, photo, and back-navigation. Used on profile/expert pages

content_model.structure: Single row: Col1 = background image, Col2 = heading + subtitle + CTA link (as strong/em link for button styling)

rows.col2: h1 heading, paragraph, CTA link (bold = primary button)

content_model.notes: Block Collection hero uses image in first column, content in second. For carousel variant, use a Carousel block wrapping multiple hero content panels.

blocks.complexity_notes: CSS-only for static hero. Carousel variant needs Block Collection Carousel JS.

blocks.name: cards
blocks.description: Grid of content cards displaying items with images, headings, descriptions, and optional CTAs. The most versatile block on the site with 5 distinct variants used across all major archetypes. The EDS Block Collection Cards block supports variant classes to differentiate layouts.

variants.name: cards (default)
variants.description: Standard link-box cards: image on top, h4 title, description text, 'Find out more' CTA. 2-3 column responsive grid. Used for service divisions, about sub-pages, and general navigation.

variants.name: cards (news)
variants.description: News cards: image on top, date below, then article title. Used for 'Latest News' sections on division landing pages and homepage. Sometimes in a carousel.

variants.name: cards (resource)
variants.description: Knowledge resource cards: dark gradient overlay on background image, resource type tag (e.g., 'White Paper', 'Webinar'), title overlay. 3-column grid. Links to knowledge library items.

variants.name: cards (event)
variants.description: Event cards: image, event name, date range, location text, short description, 'Event Details' CTA. 3-column grid. Used on event listing and division pages.

variants.name: cards (related)
variants.description: Related content cards: compact row of 2-3 cards with type tag, title, optional author name. Placed at the bottom of article/detail pages for cross-linking to related content.

content_model.structure: Each card is one row. Col1 = image, Col2 = text content (heading, paragraph, link). Variant class on block name determines layout. For 'related' variant, content is auto-populated from query-index.

variant_class_mapping.cards (news): Date-first layout, auto-populated from news query-index

content_model.notes: News, resource, and related variants are best auto-populated from query-index.json rather than manually authored per page. The block JS fetches from the index and renders dynamically.

blocks.complexity_notes: Base cards block is CSS-only from Block Collection. News/resource/related/event variants need light JS for query-index fetching and variant-specific rendering.

blocks.name: columns
blocks.description: Two-column layout with one side containing an image (or video thumbnail) and the other side containing text with heading, paragraphs, and optional CTA button. Alternates left/right alignment on successive instances within a page. The core layout pattern for service description content.

variants.name: columns (default)
variants.description: 50/50 split image + text. Standard media-text layout.

variants.name: columns (video)
variants.description: Same as default but with a video thumbnail + play button in the media column. Clicking opens video modal.

content_model.structure: Single row: Col1 = image or video thumbnail, Col2 = heading + paragraphs + optional CTA link
content_model.notes: Block Collection columns handles N columns per row. For media-text pattern, always 2 columns. Alternating left/right can be achieved with CSS :nth-child or section metadata style class.

blocks.complexity_notes: Pure CSS layout from Block Collection. No custom JS needed.

blocks.name: carousel
blocks.description: Rotating content display used for hero slides on the homepage and for testimonial rotation. The source site uses Slick.js. EDS Block Collection has a native Carousel block.

variants.name: carousel (hero)
variants.description: Homepage hero carousel with multiple banner slides, each containing background image + text overlay + CTA

variants.name: carousel (testimonial)
variants.description: Rotating testimonial quotes. Uses carousel to cycle through customer/partner quotes.

content_model.structure: Each slide is one row. Content depends on what's inside each slide (hero content or testimonial quote).

rows.col1: Slide content: image + text or quote text + attribution

content_model.notes: Block Collection Carousel wraps each row as a slide. For hero carousel, each row has bg image + text content. For testimonial carousel, each row has quote text + author.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Carousel provides JS for slide navigation, indicators, and touch/swipe. CSS customization needed for hero variant styling.

blocks.name: video
blocks.description: Video embed for Vimeo and YouTube content. The source site uses modal popup playback (click thumbnail → video plays in overlay). EDS Block Collection has both Video block (direct playback) and Embed block (social/third-party), plus Modal autoblock.

variants.name: video (inline)
variants.description: Video plays inline on the page within a columns or standalone section

variants.name: video (modal)
variants.description: Video thumbnail with play button; clicking opens modal overlay for playback. Common pattern on division landing pages.

content_model.structure: Single row: Col1 = video URL (Vimeo/YouTube link). For modal variant, link text or poster image triggers modal.
content_model.notes: Block Collection Video handles direct video files; Embed handles Vimeo/YouTube. For modal playback, combine with Modal autoblock (link to fragment containing the embed). Consider using Embed block for Vimeo/YouTube, Video for self-hosted mp4.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Video/Embed + Modal provide the foundation. Modal variant requires Fragment + Modal autoblock pattern.

blocks.name: embed
blocks.description: Embeds for third-party content including Vimeo/YouTube videos and other external widgets. Used alongside Video block for iframe-based embeds.

variants.name: embed (video)
variants.description: Vimeo/YouTube video embed

variants.name: embed (map)
variants.description: Google Maps embed for office locations

content_model.structure: Single row: Col1 = URL to embed
content_model.notes: Block Collection Embed auto-detects provider from URL and renders appropriate iframe. Acts as autoblock when a bare URL is on its own line.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Embed handles provider detection. Autoblock capability means authors just paste a URL.

blocks.name: quote
blocks.description: Client/partner testimonials and pull quotes. The source site uses a testimonial carousel with quote text and attribution. EDS Block Collection Quote provides the individual quote structure.

source_pattern.html_pattern:
Quote text

Author Name, Title

variants.name: quote (default)
variants.description: Single testimonial or pull quote with text and attribution

content_model.structure: Row 1: Col1 = quote text. Row 2 (optional): Col1 = attribution (author name, title, company)
content_model.notes: For multiple testimonials, wrap Quote blocks inside a Carousel block. Block Collection Quote provides blockquote styling.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Quote is CSS-only. For carousel of quotes, combine with Carousel block.

blocks.name: accordion
blocks.description: Expandable/collapsible content sections. Not heavily used on the current site but useful for FAQ-style content that may exist in knowledge library articles and service detail pages.

variants.name: accordion (default)
variants.description: Standard expandable Q&A or collapsible content panels

content_model.structure: Each row is one accordion item. Col1 = heading/label (question), Col2 = content (answer/details)
content_model.notes: Block Collection Accordion handles expand/collapse JS and accessibility (aria-expanded). Each row becomes one collapsible panel.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Accordion provides JS + CSS. No customization needed.

blocks.name: tabs
blocks.description: Tabbed content panels for organizing related information. Used on some service pages where content is grouped by category (e.g., capabilities by phase). EDS Block Collection Tabs handles this natively.

variants.name: tabs (default)
variants.description: Horizontal tab navigation with content panels

content_model.structure: Each row is one tab. Col1 = tab label, Col2 = tab content (can contain other blocks via fragments)
content_model.notes: Block Collection Tabs provides tab switching JS and accessibility. Content can include any default content or nested blocks via Fragment references.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Tabs provides all needed JS + CSS.

blocks.name: breadcrumbs
blocks.description: Breadcrumb navigation trail showing the user's position in the site hierarchy. Present on 22 of 32 sampled pages. The EDS Block Collection includes Breadcrumbs as a 'Block Add-on' and the Block Party has a community implementation.

variants.name: breadcrumbs (default)
variants.description: Auto-generated breadcrumb trail based on URL hierarchy. Text-only, horizontal.

content_model.structure: Auto-generated from page URL hierarchy. No author input needed — the block reads the site structure and builds the breadcrumb trail automatically.
content_model.notes: Block Collection Breadcrumbs is a block add-on (auto-blocked). It should be implemented in scripts.js to automatically inject breadcrumbs based on page path. No authoring table needed.

blocks.complexity_notes: Needs JS to read page hierarchy from query-index or URL path and build breadcrumb DOM. Block Collection provides reference implementation.

blocks.name: hubspot-form
blocks.description: HubSpot embedded form for lead capture, content gating, contact requests, and newsletter signup. Present on virtually every page (30 of 32 sampled). The site-wide form appears to be a persistent CTA/contact form, while some pages have additional page-specific forms with more fields.

variants.name: hubspot-form (site-wide)
variants.description: Standard 8-field contact/inquiry form that appears on most pages, typically near the footer. Same form ID across pages.

variants.name: hubspot-form (gated-content)
variants.description: Page-specific form for gated content access (whitepapers, webinars). Has 10-11 fields including company, role, interest area.

variants.name: hubspot-form (product-inquiry)
variants.description: Detailed product inquiry form with up to 19 fields. Used on product detail pages for sample requests.

content_model.structure: Single row: Col1 = HubSpot portal ID, Col2 = HubSpot form ID. The block loads the HubSpot embed script and creates the form.
content_model.notes: This is a custom block. Authors provide the HubSpot portal and form IDs; the block JS loads the HubSpot Forms API script and initializes the form. The site-wide form could be implemented as a Fragment in the footer to avoid repeating on every page.

blocks.complexity_notes: Custom block loading HubSpot Forms JS API. Straightforward but external dependency. Consider lazy-loading in delayed.js for performance.

blocks.name: stats
blocks.description: Row of 4-5 key statistics displayed as large animated numbers with descriptive labels below. Used on the homepage to showcase company metrics (e.g., '6000+ employees', '50+ years', '19 global sites').

variants.name: stats (default)
variants.description: Horizontal row of stat items with animated number counters that increment on scroll into view

content_model.structure: Each row is one stat. Col1 = number (with suffix like '+' or 'M+'), Col2 = label/description.
content_model.notes: Custom block. Animation effect (counter increment) is a nice-to-have JS enhancement, not critical. Core content is just number + label pairs.

blocks.complexity_notes: CSS layout is simple. Animated counter requires IntersectionObserver + requestAnimationFrame JS. Can be deferred to delayed.js.

blocks.name: profile
blocks.description: Professional profile display with person's photo, name, title/role, bio text, and contact/social links. Used for knowledge experts, business development team members, and expert profiles.

variants.name: profile (expert)
variants.description: Knowledge expert profile with photo, name, credentials, full bio, areas of expertise list, and contact form

variants.name: profile (bd-team)
variants.description: Business development team member profile with photo, name, region, bio, contact details, and upcoming events

content_model.structure: Row 1: Col1 = photo image, Col2 = name (h2) + title/role (paragraph) + credentials. Additional rows for bio sections are default content below the block.
content_model.notes: The profile block handles the hero-like header portion. Bio content, expertise lists, and related sections below are default content and other blocks (cards for events, hubspot-form for contact). Consider using Columns block for the photo + info layout and keeping bio as default content.

blocks.complexity_notes: Primarily CSS layout. No complex JS needed. Photo + text layout is straightforward.

blocks.name: fragment
blocks.description: Reusable content fragment that can be embedded across multiple pages. Essential for shared components like the site-wide HubSpot form, footer CTAs, and common content sections.

source_pattern.html_pattern: N/A — no direct source-site equivalent; this is an EDS architecture pattern

variants.name: fragment (default)
variants.description: References a separate content page whose content is inlined at this position

content_model.structure: Single row, single cell: URL/path to the fragment page
content_model.notes: Used to DRY-up repeated content across pages. Key use cases: site-wide HubSpot form section, footer CTA, common testimonial blocks.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Fragment fetches and inlines content. No customization needed.

blocks.name: search
blocks.description: Site-wide search functionality. The source site uses WordPress search. EDS Block Collection includes a Search block that works with query-index.

variants.name: search (default)
variants.description: Search input that queries the EDS query-index and displays results

content_model.structure: Typically placed in the header or as a standalone page. No authoring table needed — block reads from query-index.
content_model.notes: Block Collection Search works with query-index.json. Implementation is in header or dedicated search page.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Search provides baseline. May need customization for Almac's multi-site structure.

blocks.name: content-filter
blocks.description: Faceted filter/listing interface for browsable content collections. Used on events listing, news listing, and knowledge library pages. Provides dropdown and checkbox filters with dynamic result display.

variants.name: content-filter (events)
variants.description: Event listing with filters for date range, location, division/service area. 928 event pages in manifest.

variants.name: content-filter (news)
variants.description: News listing with filters for division, date, category. 679 news posts in manifest.

variants.name: content-filter (knowledge)
variants.description: Knowledge library listing with filters for content type (whitepaper, webinar, etc.), topic area, expert. 723 items in manifest.

content_model.structure: Configuration block specifying which query-index sheet to use and which metadata fields to expose as filters. Row 1: Col1 = 'source', Col2 = query-index sheet name. Row 2+: Col1 = filter field name, Col2 = filter type (dropdown/checkbox/daterange).
content_model.notes: Most complex custom block. Reads from query-index.json, applies client-side filtering, renders card results with pagination. Block Party's Content Filter Hub provides a reference architecture. Must support URL-based filter state for shareability.

blocks.complexity_notes: Significant JS required: query-index fetching, client-side filtering, result rendering, pagination, URL state management. The Block Party Content Filter Hub can serve as a reference implementation.

blocks.name: sidebar
blocks.description: Sidebar layout with navigation links, category filters, or related content. Used on detail pages (news articles, product pages, career stories) to provide contextual navigation.

variants.name: sidebar (navigation)
variants.description: Vertical navigation menu for sub-page hierarchy. Used on product detail and sub-service pages.

variants.name: sidebar (article)
variants.description: Article sidebar with related posts, share buttons, and category links. Used on news posts and career stories.

content_model.structure: Implemented as a section style rather than a block. Use Section Metadata with 'style: sidebar' to create a two-column layout where the sidebar section sits alongside the main content section.
content_model.notes: In EDS, sidebars are best implemented as section styles using CSS Grid, not as blocks. The main content and sidebar are separate sections on the page; Section Metadata with 'style: sidebar' triggers the layout. Sidebar content is authored as normal blocks/default content within that section.

blocks.complexity_notes: CSS-only section style using CSS Grid for the two-column layout. No JS needed. The challenge is content authoring — authors must understand section boundaries.

blocks.name: modal
blocks.description: Popup overlay for video playback, form display, or content previews. The source site uses modals for video playback and gated content forms. EDS Block Collection includes Modal as an autoblock.

variants.name: modal (video)
variants.description: Video playback in overlay

variants.name: modal (form)
variants.description: Gated content form in overlay

content_model.structure: Auto-blocked: any link to a #hash that matches a fragment ID triggers a modal. The modal content lives in a Fragment page.
content_model.notes: Block Collection Modal is an autoblock — it automatically works when a link points to a fragment with a hash. Authors create a Fragment page with the modal content, then link to it with #fragment-id.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Modal autoblock handles everything. No custom code needed.

blocks.name: contact-locations
blocks.description: Contact page layout with a contact form on one side and office location cards on the other. Each location card shows office image, address, phone number, and map link.

variants.name: contact-locations (default)
variants.description: Two-column layout: contact form (HubSpot) on left, 3 office location cards on right

content_model.structure: Can be achieved using Columns block: Col1 = HubSpot Form fragment, Col2 = location cards (or Cards block). Alternatively, dedicated block with: Row 1 = form config, Rows 2-4 = location cards (image | name, address, phone, link).

rows.col2: Office name (h3), Address (p), Phone (p), Map link

content_model.notes: Rather than building a custom block, compose from existing blocks: use a Columns block where one column contains a Fragment (HubSpot form) and the other contains Cards with office details. This follows the EDS philosophy of composition over custom blocks.

blocks.complexity_notes: Achievable through composition of Columns + Fragment + Cards blocks. No custom JS.

blocks.name: icon-grid
blocks.description: Grid of service/feature icons with labels. Used on the Clinical Technologies page to showcase technology capabilities. Can be implemented as a Cards variant with icon images.

variants.name: icon-grid (default)
variants.description: Grid of small icons with text labels, used for feature/capability lists

content_model.structure: Implement as Cards (icon) variant. Each row: Col1 = icon image (small SVG/PNG), Col2 = label text.
content_model.notes: Rather than a dedicated block, use the Cards block with a variant class 'cards (icon)' and CSS to render small icons in a grid. This keeps the block palette minimal.

blocks.complexity_notes: CSS-only variant of Cards block.

blocks.name: table
blocks.description: Data table for structured information like product specifications, comparison charts, or regulatory data. EDS Block Collection includes a Table block.

variants.name: table (default)
variants.description: Standard data table with headers, used for product specifications

content_model.structure: Row 1 = header row. Subsequent rows = data rows. Column count matches data structure.
content_model.notes: Block Collection Table handles responsive display. First row is treated as header.

blocks.complexity_notes: Block Collection Table is CSS-only with optional responsive JS.

section_styles.name: sidebar
section_styles.description: Two-column layout with main content (wide) and sidebar (narrow). Applied via Section Metadata 'style: sidebar'.
section_styles.css_approach: CSS Grid with 2fr 1fr columns on desktop, stack on mobile

section_styles.name: full-width-image
section_styles.description: Section with full-width background image and optional text/CTA overlay. Applied via Section Metadata 'style: full-width-image' with 'background' property for the image URL.
section_styles.css_approach: Background image with cover sizing, text overlay positioned center/bottom

section_styles.name: cta-band
section_styles.description: Colored background section with centered heading and CTA button. Used for call-to-action sections between content blocks.
section_styles.css_approach: Accent background color, centered text, button styling

section_styles.name: dark
section_styles.description: Dark background section for contrast. Used for stats, testimonials, and featured content areas.
section_styles.css_approach: Dark background with light text colors

section_styles.name: grey
section_styles.description: Light grey background for alternating section contrast.

default_content_patterns.description: Paragraphs, headings (h1-h5), lists (ul/ol), and inline images. The majority of article/detail page content is default content.
Call-to-action buttons rendered as bold/emphasized links. 'Find out more', 'Learn more', 'Contact us', 'Download' patterns.
default_content_patterns.eds_treatment:
Links styled as buttons:

CTA Text

for primary,

CTA Text

for secondary

default_content_patterns.description: Section introduction headings (h2) with optional subtitle paragraph. Used to introduce each content section.
default_content_patterns.eds_treatment: Default content — h2 heading + paragraph at the start of each section

default_content_patterns.description: Pull quotes and highlighted text passages within article content.
default_content_patterns.eds_treatment: Default content — <blockquote> tags styled via CSS

key_decisions: Sidebar is a section style, not a block — avoids authoring complexity
Cards has 5 variants rather than 5 separate blocks — minimizes palette while covering all patterns
News/resource/event/related card variants auto-populate from query-index — reduces manual authoring
HubSpot form block is custom since Block Collection Form is deprecated
Site-wide HubSpot form should be a Fragment to avoid duplication across 3500+ pages
Icon grid is a Cards variant, not a separate block
Contact-locations page uses Columns + Fragment + Cards composition rather than custom block
Breadcrumbs are auto-blocked from URL hierarchy, not manually authored
Content-filter is the single most complex custom block — serves events (928), news (679), and knowledge (723) listings
